crime.value: Child kidnapping and shooting during rescue attempt
crime.quote: I raised the gun again and pointed at his chest and fired his face changed to an ashen colour

victim.value: Tony, who kidnapped the narrator's daughter Emma to raise as his own child
victim.quote: Dont come looking for me Tony or I will kill you, next time find your own baby you are not haveing mine

closed_world.value: Tony's house where Emma was being held captive
closed_world.quote: I left them both tied up and went upstairs, I heard laughter in the middle room

culprit_and_motive.value: The narrator (Emma's mother) shoots Tony to rescue her kidnapped daughter
culprit_and_motive.quote: I had not gone through all of this to lose my child

resolution.value: Mother successfully rescues daughter, flees to Spain to start new life, disposes of evidence
resolution.quote: I told her I had got rid of all evidence that linked me with Tony, the gun had gone in the river

red_herring.value: David initially appears as potential threat/antagonist but is revealed as victim of Tony's deception
red_herring.quote: I loved David and after the holiday we were closer than ever, the look of pain on his face was awful

social_world.value: Domestic/familial power structures - custody battles, parental rights, family loyalty networks
social_world.quote: my mum organised a flight to Benidorm, she booked me on a package holiday

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical confrontation and shooting reveal Tony's true character and resolve the custody conflict
reveal_mechanic.quote: I raised the gun again and pointed at his chest and fired his face changed to an ashen colour

investigator_wound.value: Maternal trauma - protagonist's vulnerability as mother whose child has been taken
investigator_wound.quote: I had not gone through all of this to lose my child
